title: How to Build Professional Video Animations with Claude.
summary: Tutorial on using Claude Code and Remotion to generate professional video animations from text prompts. Covers installation, visual style configuration, plan mode workflow, and end-to-end animation creation in approximately ten minutes without manual editing.
writeup: This video walks through a solo-creator workflow for producing professional motion-graphics animations without a video editor: the host records a talking-head video, passes it to Claude Code, which runs Whisper AI to extract word-level timing and context, then generates Remotion components that animate each section of the narration. The practical through-line is that a single natural-language prompt, reinforced by a persistent style file and quality-anchor language, can replace an entire motion-design step. The result is a full library of timed animation clips the creator picks from during editing rather than scrambling to produce.
key_points: Claude Code uses Whisper AI internally to transcribe the source video, extracting not just word-level timing but the contextual meaning of each section, so the generated Remotion animations match both pacing and argument rather than serving as generic overlays.

Enabling plan mode in Claude Code (Shift+Tab before any prompt) is presented as the single most impactful procedural step: it forces Claude to plan the full animation structure before writing any code, consistently producing better output than prompting without it.

A persistent style file, created once and referenced in every subsequent prompt, ensures visual consistency across all videos; the style is seeded with a quality-anchor phrase such as "looks like it cost $100,000," which Claude interprets as a concrete standard to meet.

Remotion renders a live browser preview of each animation component, allowing iterative refinement through specific natural-language feedback (for example, "the text is overlapping the graph") before a final MP4 render is triggered.
The workflow produces animations for every section of a video, not just highlights, giving the editor a full library of clips to draw from rather than requiring on-demand production mid-edit.
